source: CLASS PreK Manual (Hamre, La Paro, Pianta, 2008) via Grow by Level Data

PK1.name: Positive Climate
PK1.definition: Reflects the emotional connection between the teacher and students and among students and the warmth, respect, and enjoyment communicated by verbal and nonverbal interactions

Relationships.low: There are few, if any, indications that the teacher and students enjoy warm, supportive relationships with one another
Relationships.mid: There are some indications that the teacher and students enjoy warm, supportive relationships with one another
Relationships.high: There are many indications that the teacher and students enjoy warm, supportive relationships with one another

Positive Affect.low: There are no or few displays of positive affect by the teacher and/or students
Positive Affect.mid: There are sometimes displays of positive affect by the teacher and/or students
Positive Affect.high: There are frequent displays of positive affect by the teacher and/or students

Positive Communication.low: There are rarely positive communications, verbal or physical, among teachers and students
Positive Communication.mid: There are sometimes positive communications, verbal or physical, among teachers and students
Positive Communication.high: There are frequently positive communications, verbal or physical, among teachers and students

Respect.low: The teacher and students rarely, if ever, demonstrate respect for one another
Respect.mid: The teacher and students sometimes demonstrate respect for one another
Respect.high: The teacher and students consistently demonstrate respect for one another

PK2.name: Negative Climate
PK2.definition: Reflects the overall level of expressed negativity in the classroom; the frequency, quality, and intensity of teacher and peer negativity are key to this scale
PK2.note: Negative Climate is scaled in the opposite direction. Higher negativity indicates lower quality.

Negative Affect.low: The teacher and students do not display strong negative affect and only rarely, if ever, display mild negativity
Negative Affect.mid: The classroom is characterized by mild displays of irritability, anger, or other negative affect by the teacher and/or students
Negative Affect.high: The classroom is characterized by consistent irritability, anger, or other negative affect by the teacher and/or the students

Punitive Control.low: The teacher does not yell or make threats to establish control
Punitive Control.mid: The teacher occasionally uses expressed negativity such as threats or yelling to establish control
Punitive Control.high: The teacher repeatedly yells at students or makes threats to establish control

Sarcasm/Disrespect.low: The teacher and students are not sarcastic or disrespectful
Sarcasm/Disrespect.mid: The teacher and/or students are occasionally sarcastic or disrespectful
Sarcasm/Disrespect.high: The teacher and/or students are repeatedly sarcastic or disrespectful

Severe Negativity.low: There are no instances of severe negativity between the teacher and students
Severe Negativity.mid: There are no instances of severe negativity between the teacher and students
Severe Negativity.high: There are instances of severe negativity between the teacher and students or among the students

PK3.name: Teacher Sensitivity
PK3.definition: Encompasses the teacher's awareness of and responsivity to students' academic and emotional needs; high levels of sensitivity facilitate students' ability to actively explore and learn because the teacher consistently provides comfort, reassurance, and encouragement

markers: Notices lack of understanding and/or difficulties
Awareness.low: The teacher consistently fails to be aware of students who need extra support, assistance, or attention
Awareness.mid: The teacher is sometimes aware of students who need extra support, assistance, or attention
Awareness.high: The teacher is consistently aware of students who need extra support, assistance, or attention

Responsiveness.low: The teacher is unresponsive to or dismissive of students and provides the same level of assistance to all students, regardless of their individual needs
Responsiveness.mid: The teacher is responsive to students sometimes but at other times is more dismissive or unresponsive, matching support to the needs of some students but not others
Responsiveness.high: The teacher is consistently responsive to students and matches support to their needs and abilities

markers: Helps in an effective and timely manner
Addresses Problems.low: The teacher is ineffective at addressing students' problems and concerns
Addresses Problems.mid: The teacher is sometimes effective at addressing students' problems and concerns
Addresses Problems.high: The teacher is consistently effective at addressing students' problems and concerns

Student Comfort.low: The students rarely seek support, share their ideas with, or respond to questions from the teacher
Student Comfort.mid: The students sometimes seek support from, share their ideas with, or respond to questions from the teacher
Student Comfort.high: The students appear comfortable seeking support from, sharing their ideas with, and responding freely to the teacher

PK4.name: Regard for Student Perspectives
PK4.definition: Captures the degree to which the teacher's interactions with students and classroom activities place an emphasis on students' interests, motivations, and points of view and encourage student responsibility and autonomy

Flexibility and Student Focus.low: The teacher is rigid, inflexible, and controlling; most classroom activities are teacher-driven
Flexibility and Student Focus.mid: The teacher may follow the students' lead during some periods and be more controlling during others
Flexibility and Student Focus.high: The teacher is flexible, goes along with students' ideas, and organizes instruction around students' interests

Support for Autonomy and Leadership.low: The teacher does not support student autonomy and leadership
Support for Autonomy and Leadership.mid: The teacher sometimes provides support for student autonomy and leadership but at other times fails to do so
Support for Autonomy and Leadership.high: The teacher provides consistent support for student autonomy and leadership

Student Expression.low: There are few opportunities for student talk and expression
Student Expression.mid: There are periods with a lot of student talk and expression but other times when teacher talk predominates
Student Expression.high: There are many opportunities for student talk and expression

Restriction of Movement.low: The teacher is highly controlling of students' movement and placement during activities
Restriction of Movement.mid: The teacher is somewhat controlling of students' movement and placement during activities
Restriction of Movement.high: The students have freedom of movement and placement during activities

PK5.name: Behavior Management
PK5.definition: Encompasses the teacher's ability to provide clear behavioral expectations and use effective methods to prevent and redirect misbehavior

Clear Behavior Expectations.low: Rules and expectations are absent, unclear, or inconsistently enforced
Clear Behavior Expectations.mid: Rules and expectations may be stated clearly but are inconsistently enforced
Clear Behavior Expectations.high: Rules and expectations for behavior are clear and consistently enforced

Proactive.low: The teacher is reactive, and monitoring is absent or ineffective
Proactive.mid: The teacher uses a mix of proactive and reactive responses; sometimes monitors and reacts to early indicators but other times misses or ignores them
Proactive.high: The teacher is consistently proactive and monitors the classroom effectively to prevent problems from developing

Redirection of Misbehavior.low: Attempts to redirect misbehavior are ineffective; the teacher rarely focuses on positives or uses subtle cues. Misbehavior continues and/or escalates
Redirection of Misbehavior.mid: Some attempts to redirect are effective, particularly when focusing on positive behavior and using subtle cues. Few times when misbehavior continues or escalates
Redirection of Misbehavior.high: The teacher effectively redirects misbehavior by focusing on positives and using subtle cues. Behavior management does not take time away from learning

Student Behavior.low: There are frequent instances of misbehavior in the classroom
Student Behavior.mid: There are periodic episodes of misbehavior in the classroom
Student Behavior.high: There are few, if any, instances of student misbehavior in the classroom

PK6.name: Productivity
PK6.definition: Considers how well the teacher manages instructional time and routines and provides activities for students so that they have the opportunity to be involved in learning activities

Maximizing Learning Time.low: Few, if any, activities are provided for students, and an excessive amount of time is spent addressing disruptions and completing managerial tasks
Maximizing Learning Time.mid: The teacher provides activities most of the time, but some learning time is lost in dealing with disruptions and managerial tasks
Maximizing Learning Time.high: The teacher provides activities for the students and deals efficiently with disruptions and managerial tasks

Routines.low: The classroom routines are unclear; most students do not know what is expected of them
Routines.mid: There is some evidence of classroom routines that allow everyone to know what is expected
Routines.high: The classroom resembles a 'well-oiled machine'; everybody knows what is expected and how to go about doing it

Transitions.low: Transitions are too long, too frequent, and/or inefficient
Transitions.mid: Transitions sometimes take too long or are too frequent and inefficient

Preparation.low: The teacher does not have activities prepared and ready for the students
Preparation.mid: The teacher is mostly prepared but takes some time away from instruction for last-minute preparations
Preparation.high: The teacher is fully prepared for activities and lessons

PK7.name: Instructional Learning Formats
PK7.definition: Focuses on the ways in which the teacher maximizes students' interest, engagement, and ability to learn from lessons and activities

Effective Facilitation.low: The teacher does not actively facilitate activities and lessons to encourage students' interest and expanded involvement
Effective Facilitation.mid: At times, the teacher actively facilitates but at other times merely provides activities for the students
Effective Facilitation.high: The teacher actively facilitates students' engagement in activities and lessons to encourage participation and expanded involvement

markers: Range of auditory, visual, and movement opportunities
Variety of Modalities and Materials.low: The teacher does not use a variety of modalities or materials to gain students' interest and participation
Variety of Modalities and Materials.mid: The teacher is inconsistent in use of a variety of modalities and materials
Variety of Modalities and Materials.high: The teacher uses a variety of modalities including auditory, visual, and movement and uses a variety of materials to effectively interest students

Student Interest.low: The students do not appear interested and/or involved in the lesson or activities
Student Interest.mid: Students may be engaged for periods of time, but at other times their interest wanes
Student Interest.high: Students are consistently interested and involved in activities and lessons

Clarity of Learning Objectives.low: The teacher makes no attempt or is unsuccessful at orienting students toward learning objectives
Clarity of Learning Objectives.mid: The teacher orients students somewhat, or learning objectives may be clear during some periods but less so during others
Clarity of Learning Objectives.high: The teacher effectively focuses students' attention toward learning objectives and/or the purpose of the lesson

PK8.name: Concept Development
PK8.definition: Measures the teacher's use of instructional discussions and activities to promote students' higher-order thinking skills and cognition and the teacher's focus on understanding rather than on rote instruction

Analysis and Reasoning.low: The teacher rarely uses discussions and activities that encourage analysis and reasoning
Analysis and Reasoning.mid: The teacher occasionally uses discussions and activities that encourage analysis and reasoning
Analysis and Reasoning.high: The teacher often uses discussions and activities that encourage analysis and reasoning

Creating.low: The teacher rarely provides opportunities for students to be creative and/or generate their own ideas and products
Creating.mid: The teacher sometimes provides opportunities for students to be creative and/or generate their own ideas and products
Creating.high: The teacher often provides opportunities for students to be creative and/or generate their own ideas and products

Integration.low: Concepts and activities are presented independent of one another, and students are not asked to apply previous learning
Integration.mid: The teacher sometimes links concepts and activities to one another and to previous learning
Integration.high: The teacher consistently links concepts and activities to one another and to previous learning

Connections to the Real World.low: The teacher does not relate concepts to the students' actual lives
Connections to the Real World.mid: The teacher makes some attempts to relate concepts to the students' actual lives
Connections to the Real World.high: The teacher consistently relates concepts to the students' actual lives

PK9.name: Quality of Feedback
PK9.definition: Assesses the degree to which the teacher provides feedback that expands learning and understanding and encourages continued participation

Scaffolding.low: The teacher rarely provides scaffolding but rather dismisses responses as incorrect or ignores problems in understanding
Scaffolding.mid: The teacher occasionally provides scaffolding but at other times simply dismisses responses as incorrect
Scaffolding.high: The teacher often scaffolds for students who are having a hard time understanding a concept, answering a question, or completing an activity

Feedback Loops.low: The teacher gives only perfunctory feedback to students
Feedback Loops.mid: There are occasional feedback loops between the teacher and students; other times feedback is more perfunctory
Feedback Loops.high: There are frequent feedback loops — back-and-forth exchanges — between the teacher and students

Prompting Thought Processes.low: The teacher rarely queries students or prompts them to explain their thinking and rationale
Prompting Thought Processes.mid: The teacher occasionally queries students or prompts them to explain their thinking
Prompting Thought Processes.high: The teacher often queries students or prompts them to explain their thinking and rationale

Providing Information.low: The teacher rarely provides additional information to expand on students' understanding or actions
Providing Information.mid: The teacher occasionally provides additional information to expand on students' understanding
Providing Information.high: The teacher often provides additional information to expand on students' understanding or actions

Encouragement and Affirmation.low: The teacher rarely offers encouragement of students' efforts that increases involvement and persistence
Encouragement and Affirmation.mid: The teacher occasionally offers encouragement that increases involvement and persistence
Encouragement and Affirmation.high: The teacher often offers encouragement of students' efforts that increases involvement and persistence

PK10.name: Language Modeling
PK10.definition: Captures the quality and amount of the teacher's use of language-stimulation and language-facilitation techniques

Frequent Conversation.low: There are few if any conversations in the classroom
Frequent Conversation.mid: There are limited conversations in the classroom
Frequent Conversation.high: There are frequent conversations in the classroom

markers: Questions require more than a one-word response
Open-Ended Questions.low: The majority of the teacher's questions are closed-ended
Open-Ended Questions.mid: The teacher asks a mix of closed-ended and open-ended questions
Open-Ended Questions.high: The teacher asks many open-ended questions

Repetition and Extension.low: The teacher rarely, if ever, repeats or extends the students' responses
Repetition and Extension.mid: The teacher sometimes repeats or extends the students' responses
Repetition and Extension.high: The teacher often repeats or extends the students' responses

Self- and Parallel Talk.low: The teacher rarely maps his or her own actions and the students' actions through language and description
Self- and Parallel Talk.mid: The teacher occasionally maps his or her own actions and the students' actions through language
Self- and Parallel Talk.high: The teacher consistently maps his or her own actions and the students' actions through language and description

markers: Connected to familiar words and/or ideas
Advanced Language.low: The teacher does not use advanced language with students
Advanced Language.mid: The teacher sometimes uses advanced language with students
Advanced Language.high: The teacher often uses advanced language with students
